name: MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification Scheme
administered_by: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (Quality Council of India)
retrieval_text: Certification-cost subsidy for MSMEs adopting Zero Defect Zero Effect (ZED) quality and sustainability practices at Bronze, Silver, or Gold level: a base subsidy of 80% for micro, 60% for small, and 50% for medium enterprises on certification cost, with an additional 10% for units owned by women or SC/ST entrepreneurs or located in NER/Himalayan/LWE/island/aspirational districts, plus a further 5% for units in SFURTI or MSE-CDP clusters. A one-time Rs 10,000 joining reward is credited on taking the ZED Pledge. This scheme has no loan component; it reimburses certification costs directly and carries no interest rate.
